name: navigation-menu
title: Navigation Menu
description: A site navigation menu built on Base UI's Navigation Menu: a bar of top-level destinations where some items open a disclosure / mega-menu panel of grouped links. Follows the APG disclosure-navigation contract — panels open on click, Enter, Space, the orientation's arrow key, or hover-intent (never hover-only), Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger. Renders a real nav landmark, marks the current page with aria-current, reuses the Commons Link contract for links, flips logical sides in RTL, and keeps 44px targets and visible borders for forced-colors mode.
useWhen: Primary site navigation for a government or content site (a top bar of destinations)
A top-level item that reveals a mega-menu panel of grouped links with optional descriptions
Navigation that must work by keyboard and touch, not just hover
A stacked side navigation (set orientation to vertical)
avoidWhen: A menu of application actions revealed from a trigger (use a Dropdown Menu)
A single on/off control (use a Button or a Switch)
A long, filterable set of choices bound to a form value (use the Combo Box)
A simple flat row of links with no panels (a plain nav landmark with links is enough)

keyboard: Tab moves between top-level triggers and links; Shift+Tab moves back, and Tab past the bar leaves it
The trigger exposes aria-expanded and aria-controls; Enter or Space opens and closes its panel
The orientation's arrow key opens a panel and moves focus into it (Down Arrow for a horizontal bar; the inline-end arrow for a vertical one)
Arrow keys move between links inside an open panel
Escape closes the open panel and returns focus to its trigger
Pointer users get hover-intent open (with a safe-polygon path to the panel); hover is never required — every panel is reachable by keyboard and touch
Moving focus out of the bar closes any open panel
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (triggers and links are min-h-11 rows)
notes: The root renders the nav landmark and takes an aria-label (or aria-labelledby); the panel popup is a nav region named by popupLabel so the two landmarks stay distinct.

The current page is marked with aria-current=page plus non-color indicators (a 2px block-end border and heavier weight on bar items, a weight bump on panel links), so it stays distinguishable in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode.
The open trigger adds weight + underline alongside its border tint, so the active item is not signalled by color alone.

Links reuse the Commons Link visual contract (always underlined — link-ness is never conveyed by color alone); the trailing trigger chevron is decorative and the open/closed state comes from aria-expanded.
The popup keeps a visible border in every state so forced-colors mode paints a boundary; the panel fade, scale, and size transitions are gated behind motion-safe.
AmbientDirection feeds the DOM dir into Base UI so logical sides and the anchored panel flip under dir=rtl like the native components.
